cases.name: golden
cases.note: the complete SDR: every commitment opened, all seven units grounded

cases.name: golden-regulator
cases.note: the §5.1 regulator projection of the SAME signed document — one signature, two projections

cases.name: proof-signature-bitflip
cases.note: signature corrupted, content untouched

cases.name: envelope-altered
cases.note: validFrom shifted; recomputed canonical hash (proof and disclosures absent) no longer matches receipt_hash

cases.name: lying-issuer-untrusted
cases.note: attacker re-hashes and re-signs under the trusted key_id with an untrusted key

cases.name: proof-stripped
cases.note: unsigned projection

cases.name: salt-swapped
cases.note: the salted commitment recomputes to a digest sd.claims never committed

cases.name: disclosure-value-altered
cases.note: disclosed successor scope widened without re-committing; the digest no longer binds

cases.name: foreign-disclosure-spliced
cases.note: a disclosure from outside the committed evidence set

cases.name: duplicate-disclosure-path
cases.note: one path opened twice

cases.name: salt-uppercase
cases.note: salts are 64 lowercase hex characters

cases.name: unknown-disclosure-path
cases.note: paths are the seven claim units or evidence/<event-uuid>

cases.name: commitments-unsorted
cases.note: commitment digests are sorted and unique; re-signed, so the wire rule itself must refuse

cases.name: spec-version-unknown
cases.note: verifiers reject spec_version values they do not implement

cases.name: evidence-tampered-hash-stale-resigned
cases.note: compromised-key model: envelope re-signed over a re-committed tampered event; the per-event hash still refuses

cases.name: evidence-signature-corrupted-resigned
cases.note: event hash intact but its signature is corrupt; re-committed and re-signed

cases.name: claim-contradicts-grant-resigned
cases.note: disclosed claim vs disclosed evidence: the grant refuses the widened scope

cases.name: validfrom-shifted-resigned
cases.note: validFrom no longer matches the disclosed completion timestamp

cases.name: hidden-obligation-resigned
cases.note: a disclosed inheritance the claimed array conceals — the concealing-holder check

cases.name: ledger-horizon-swapped-resigned
cases.note: no partial rule can see it; the complete projection escalates to the AHR horizon rule, which refuses

cases.name: active-without-validation-resigned
cases.note: validation event withheld from the commitment set itself; the complete projection escalates to the AHR claim rules, which refuse
